trajectory.description:
Starting fresh. I'll build a semantic segmentation pipeline for ADE20K using a strong pretrained backbone. Plan:
1. Use SegFormer with MIT-b5 backbone (pre-downloaded) for strong performance
2. Write model.py, train.py, predict.py
3. Train for a few epochs first to validate pipeline and measure time
4. Submit eval to get baseline score

Key decisions:
- Use MIT-b5 backbone from transformers (pre-downloaded)
- SegFormer-style decoder head
- Multi-GPU training with DataParallel
- Mixed precision training
- Proper label remapping (1-indexed → 0-indexed, 0 → 255)
- Predict at original resolution

- MISSING:	those params were newly initialized because missing from the checkpoint. Consider training on your downstream task.
trajectory.description: Observation: Error in model.py - hidden_states from MIT-b5 are 4D [B,C,H,W] not 3D [B,L,C]. The transformers library's SegFormer returns spatial feature maps directly. Analysis: Use SegformerForSemanticSegmentation from transformers which has the full pipeline built in, avoiding shape issues. Decision: Rewrite model.py to use SegformerForSemanticSegmentation directly, fix train.py and predict.py accordingly.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training was killed (exit code 137 = OOM/SIGKILL) after ~2 hours. Only 1 data point in training curves, meaning barely 1 epoch completed. No checkpoint was saved. Analysis: The training is too slow and likely OOM. Need to: (1) use DDP instead of DataParallel for better memory efficiency, (2) reduce batch size, (3) save checkpoints more frequently, (4) use gradient accumulation. Decision: Rewrite train.py with DDP, batch_size=2 per GPU, save checkpoint after each epoch, train for 3 epochs first to get a baseline.

I should submit eval first to get a baseline score, then continue training longer. Decision: Submit eval with current best checkpoint, then train for more epochs.
